title: Question: verilator's performance on a large, aggressively clock-gated design
body:
---


Author Name: **Varun Koyyalagunta**
Original Redmine Message: 980 from https://www.veripool.org



---

```

Hi all,

My company is very interested in using verilator on our design. We have
an older cycle-based simulator that we'd like to replace, and verilator
seems like the perfect solution. We also like to leverage open-source
tools as much as possible, especially ones that are as actively
maintained as verilator.

I've been conducting some tests to see how verilator compares in
simulation time. Sadly, in my test, verilator compares unfavorably to
nc-verilog, and I was wondering if there are some additional knobs or
design tweaks that could be made to speed-up the verilated model.

Some details about the subset of our design that I worked with:
  * 122k lines of verilog (not including libraries)
  * structurally written verilog
  * aggressively clock-gated
  * roughly 500k gates

I verilated the above subset of our design and fixed all non-linter
warnings. I created a set of stimulus and expected output in C++ for
verilator and and an equivalent testbench in verilog for nc-verilog.
Verilator was about 5x slower (10 seconds vs 2 seconds of simulation
time) in this test.

Verilator build notes
  * built with "verilator top.v --output-split 20000 -cc -Wno-ASSIGNIN
-Wno-WIDTH -Wno-IMPLICIT --exe sim.cpp -O3 --x-assign=fast --noassert
-CFLAGS '-O3' -LDFLAGS '-static' " (plus some '-y's for the libs)
  * takes about 8 minutes to verilate and produces 1.5million lines
of .cpp files
  * compiled with gcc version 4.4.6 in parallel on multiple machines
(VM_PARALLEL_BUILDS=1)
  * compiled with -static and -O3 (also tried -O2 and -Os, results were
similar)
  * verilator executable was 15MB
  * verlator clock_enable hint was required (model didn't simulate
correctly otherwise)

I profiled the simulation as outlined in the manual (--profile-cfuncs +
gprof). Here are the most time-consuming modules -
  % time  module
    48.49  Vtop common code
    19.27  (simple clock-gating library file)
    10.16  (top level verilog file that contains all the modules
concatenated into one file) 
    13.90  Unaccounted for/rounding error
     3.06  (simple variable-width,edge-triggered flip-flop library file)
     1.01  C++
     ...

I've also attached a copy of the stats.txt produced by --stats. 

Any guidance would be appreciated. It would be great to get verilator's
simulation time speedy enough to use it in our regressions.

Thanks,
Varun 

```
